en: One Sabbath, when he went to dine at the house of a ruler of the Pharisees, they were watching him carefully.
zh: 在安息日，耶穌進了一個法利賽人領袖的家裡吃飯，眾人都在窺探他。

en: And behold, there was a man before him who had dropsy.
zh: 正好在他面前有一個患水臌病的人；

en: And Jesus responded to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, "Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath, or not?"
zh: 耶穌對律法師和法利賽人說：“在安息日治病，可以不可以呢？”

en: But they remained silent. Then he took him and healed him and sent him away.
zh: 他們卻不出聲。耶穌扶著病人，治好他，叫他走了，

en: And he said to them, "Which of you, having a son or an ox that has fallen into a well on a Sabbath day, will not immediately pull him out?"
zh: 就對他們說：“你們中間誰的兒子或牛在安息日掉在井裡，不立刻把他拉上來呢？”

en: And they could not reply to these things.
zh: 他們不能回答這些話。

en: Now he told a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the places of honor, saying to them,
zh: 耶穌看見被邀請的客人選擇高位，就用比喻對他們說：

en: "When you are invited by someone to a wedding feast, do not sit down in a place of honor, lest someone more distinguished than you be invited by him,
zh: “你被邀請參加婚筵的時候，不要自己坐在高位上，恐怕有比你更尊貴的客人也被請來；

en: and he who invited you both will come and say to you, 'Give your place to this person,' and then you will begin with shame to take the lowest place.
zh: 那請你又請他的人過來對你說：‘請你讓位給這個人。’那時你就慚愧地退居末位了。

en: But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when your host comes he may say to you, 'Friend, move up higher.' Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at table with you.
zh: 你被邀請的時候，要坐在末位上，等請你的人過來對你說：‘朋友，請上座。’那時你在同席的人面前才有光彩。

en: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted."
zh: 因為凡高抬自己的，必要降卑；自己謙卑的，必要升高。”

en: He said also to the man who had invited him, "When you give a dinner or a banquet, do not invite your friends or your brothers or your relatives or rich neighbors, lest they also invite you in return and you be repaid.
zh: 耶穌又對邀請他的人說：“你設午餐或晚宴，不要請你的朋友、弟兄、親戚或富裕的鄰舍，恐怕他們又回請你，你就得了報答。

en: But when you give a feast, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, the blind,
zh: 你擺筵席的時候，總要邀請那貧窮的、殘廢的、瘸腿的、瞎眼的，

en: and you will be blessed, because they cannot repay you. You will be repaid at the resurrection of the just."
zh: 那你就有福了。因為他們沒有甚麼可以報答你，義人復活的時候，你必定得著報答。”

en: When one of those who reclined at table with him heard these things, he said to him, "Blessed is everyone who will eat bread in the kingdom of God!"
zh: 一起吃飯的人中有一個，聽了這些話，就對耶穌說：“在 神的國裡吃飯的人有福了。”

en: But he said to him, "A man once gave a great banquet and invited many.
zh: 耶穌對他說：“有一個人大擺筵席，請了許多客人。

en: And at the time for the banquet he sent his servant to say to those who had been invited, 'Come, for everything is now ready.'
zh: 到了開席的時候，他打發僕人去對所請的人說：‘請來吧！樣樣都準備好了。’

en: But they all alike began to make excuses. The first said to him, 'I have bought a field, and I must go out and see it. Please have me excused.'
zh: 眾人一致推辭，頭一個說：‘我買了一塊地，不得不去看一看，請原諒我。’

en: And another said, 'I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to examine them. Please have me excused.'
zh: 另一個說：‘我買了五對牛，要去試一試，請原諒我。’

en: And another said, 'I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.'
zh: 又一個說：‘我剛結了婚，不能去。’

en: So the servant came and reported these things to his master. Then the master of the house became angry and said to his servant, 'Go out quickly to the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in the poor and crippled and blind and lame.'
zh: 僕人回來把這些事告訴他主人，家主就發怒，對僕人說：‘快到城裡大街小巷去，把貧窮的、殘廢的、瞎眼的、瘸腿的，都領到這裡來。’

en: And the servant said, 'Sir, what you commanded has been done, and still there is room.'
zh: 僕人說：‘主啊，你所吩咐的已經辦了，還有空位。’

en: And the master said to the servant, 'Go out to the highways and hedges and compel people to come in, that my house may be filled.
zh: 主人就對僕人說：‘你出去到路邊籬畔，勉強人進來，好把我的屋子坐滿。

en: For I tell you, none of those men who were invited shall taste my banquet.'"
zh: 我告訴你們，先前請的那些人，一個也不得嘗我的筵席。’”

en: Now great crowds accompanied him, and he turned and said to them,
zh: 有許多人與耶穌同行，他轉身對他們說：

en: "If anyone comes to me and does not hate his own father and mother and wife and children and brothers and sisters, yes, and even his own life, he cannot be my disciple.
zh: “如果有人到我這裡來，愛我不超過愛 自己的父母、妻子、兒女、兄弟、姊妹，甚至自己的性命，就不能作我的門徒。

en: Whoever does not bear his own cross and come after me cannot be my disciple.
zh: 凡不背著自己的十字架跟隨我的，也不能作我的門徒。

en: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, does not first sit down and count the cost, whether he has enough to complete it?
zh: 你們當中有誰要蓋一座樓，不先坐下計算費用，能不能蓋成呢？

en: Otherwise, when he has laid a foundation and is not able to finish, all who see it begin to mock him,
zh: 恐怕安了地基，而不能完成，所有看見的人都譏笑他，

en: saying, 'This man began to build and was not able to finish.'
zh: 說：‘這個人開了工，卻不能完工。’

en: Or what king, going out to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and deliberate whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?
zh: 或者一個王去和別的王打仗，哪有不先坐下想想，能否用一萬兵去抵抗那領兩萬來攻打他的呢？

en: And if not, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends a delegation and asks for terms of peace.
zh: 如果不能，就該趁對方還遠的時候，派使者去談判和平的條件。

en: So therefore, any one of you who does not renounce all that he has cannot be my disciple.
zh: 這樣，你們中間不論誰，如果不撇下一切所有的，就不能作我的門徒。

en: "Salt is good, but if salt has lost its taste, how shall its saltiness be restored?
zh: “鹽本來是好的，但如果失了味，怎能使它再鹹呢？

en: It is of no use either for the soil or for the manure pile. It is thrown away. He who has ears to hear, let him hear."
zh: 或用在田裡，或放在糞裡，都不合適；只好扔在外面。有耳可聽的，就應當聽。”
